## Scientific and Benchmark Integrity

- [ ] Results are repeatable.
- [ ] Experimental assumptions are documented.
- [ ] Datasets are properly cited.
- [ ] Random seeds are documented (if applicable).
- [ ] Statistical methods are described (if applicable).

## Checklist

### Code Quality

- [ ] Code follows project style guidelines.
- [ ] Code has been self-reviewed.
- [ ] No unnecessary files are included.
- [ ] Comments have been added where appropriate.

### Testing

- [ ] Existing tests pass.
- [ ] New tests have been added where appropriate.
- [ ] CI passes successfully.

### Documentation

- [ ] Documentation has been updated.
- [ ] User-facing changes are documented.

### Security

- [ ] No known security vulnerabilities introduced.
- [ ] Sensitive information has not been committed.

### Dependencies

- [ ] No unnecessary dependencies added.
- [ ] New dependencies are documented.

### Licensing

- [ ] All added code is compatible with the project license.
- [ ] External resources are properly attributed.

- Test coverage
